proof_sketch: The race condition arises from missing __syncthreads() in the reduction loop, but it is independent of input values—only thread index and warp scheduling matter. With fixed shape (128 elements) and block size 256, the reduction strides are deterministic. All tested value distributions (including extremes, denormals, alternating signs) produced identical bitwise results, indicating that no value change can trigger a different outcome on the specific hardware.
content:
{
  "reason_category": "value_insensitive",
  "proof_sketch": "The race condition arises from missing __syncthreads() in the reduction loop, but it is independent of input values—only thread index and warp scheduling matter. With fixed shape (128 elements) and block size 256, the reduction strides are deterministic. All tested value distributions (including extremes, denormals, alternating signs) produced identical bitwise results, indicating that no value change can trigger a different outcome on the specific hardware.",
  "survival_reason": "The removed __syncthreads() inside the reduction loop creates a race condition where writes from one warp in a previous iteration may not be visible to reads from another warp in the next iteration. However, on the tested GPU, the warp scheduler consistently orders execution so that all warps complete a given stride before any warp proceeds to the next, or the shared memory subsystem ensures coherence without explicit synchronization. Since the race condition is not data-dependent—its manifestation depends solely on hardware timing—varying input values cannot force a different execution order. All tested numerical regimes (denormals, overflow-adjacent, alternating signs, etc.) yielded identical bitwise results, confirming that the mutant is unkillable under the fixed-shape contract.",
  "killable": false,
  "kill_strategy": "Unkillable: no value-level change can trigger the race condition on the tested hardware.",
  "suggested_test": null,
  "recommendations": "Even though the mutation survived, always include __syncthreads() in reduction loops for portability across different GPU architectures and driver versions. This kernel would fail on other hardware where warp scheduling does not implicitly synchronize across warps between loop iterations."
}
